cards.title: Haemoglobin
cards.summary: Always follow Hb with MCV to classify. Hb <8 g/dL = consider transfusion threshold. Symptoms matter — not just the number. Haemodilution in pregnancy lowers Hb (normal ≥10.5 in 3rd trimester).

cards.title: MCV (Mean Cell Volume)
cards.summary: Mnemonic MALT for microcytic. Normocytic = haemolysis, bleeding, renal disease, early deficiency. MCV alone is not enough — always pair with RDW and clinical context.

cards.title: MCH (Mean Cell Haemoglobin)
cards.summary: MCH closely mirrors MCV. Low MCH + low MCV = classic iron deficiency hypochromic picture. MCH alone rarely changes management but confirms the MCV pattern.

cards.title: MCHC (Mean Cell Hb Concentration)
cards.summary: MCHC >36 strongly suggests hereditary spherocytosis — the only common cause. Do osmotic fragility test and blood film. Low MCHC = hypochromic = iron deficiency until proven otherwise.

cards.title: RDW (Red Cell Distribution Width)
cards.summary: KEY differentiator: Iron deficiency = HIGH RDW. Thalassaemia trait = NORMAL RDW with low MCV. RDW rises before MCV drops in early iron deficiency — it's the earliest CBC marker of iron deficiency. Use

cards.title: WBC (White Blood Cells)
cards.summary: WBC alone is insufficient — always request DIFFERENTIAL. Neutrophils = bacterial. Lymphocytes = viral. Eosinophils = NAACP. Monocytes = TB/fungal/chronic. Basophils = CML.

cards.title: Neutrophils
cards.summary: <0.5 = severe neutropenia — high infection risk, reverse barrier nursing. Left shift (bands / metamyelocytes on film) = active infection. Clozapine: mandatory WBC monitoring. Agranulocytosis = stop dr

cards.title: Lymphocytes
cards.summary: Atypical lymphocytes on film = EBV (mono). Smear cells = CLL. CD4 <200 = AIDS-defining illness. Lymphocytosis + B symptoms + lymphadenopathy = rule out lymphoma urgently.

cards.title: Eosinophils
cards.summary: NAACP mnemonic. >0.5 = eosinophilia requiring investigation. >1.5 = hypereosinophilic syndrome — organ damage risk (cardiac, pulmonary, neurological). Eosinophilia + asthma + vasculitis = EGPA (Churg-

cards.title: Platelets
cards.summary: <50 = significant bleeding risk. <20 = spontaneous bleed risk. HIT: platelet drop >50% on heparin + thrombosis = STOP heparin, use alternative anticoagulant. TTP = schistocytes + thrombocytopenia + ne

cards.title: Reticulocytes
cards.summary: High retics + anaemia = marrow working but losing RBCs (haemolysis/bleeding). Low retics + anaemia = marrow not responding (aplasia/deficiency). Corrected reticulocyte index <2% = inadequate response

cards.title: ESR
cards.summary: ESR >100 = myeloma, malignancy, serious infection (TB, endocarditis), vasculitis until proven otherwise. Combine with CRP: both ↑ = active inflammation. ESR ↑ + CRP normal = consider myeloma (paraprot

cards.title: CRP (C-Reactive Protein)
cards.summary: CRP rises and falls FASTER than ESR — the best marker for monitoring treatment response. CRP >100 = bacterial infection or major tissue injury. CRP 5–50 = mild-moderate inflammation, non-specific. Low

cards.title: hsCRP (High-Sensitivity CRP)
cards.summary: hsCRP and standard CRP measure the SAME molecule — difference is only assay SENSITIVITY. Standard CRP detects ≥5 mg/L (infection/inflammation). hsCRP detects 0.1–10 mg/L (cardiovascular risk). FOUR KE

cards.title: ALT (Alanine Aminotransferase)
cards.summary: ALT is MORE liver-specific than AST. ALT > AST = viral/NAFLD. AST:ALT ≥2:1 = alcoholic hepatitis. ALT >1000 = ischaemic hepatitis, acute viral, or paracetamol toxicity. Always compare to baseline tren

cards.title: AST (Aspartate Aminotransferase)
cards.summary: AST:ALT ≥2:1 = alcoholic hepatitis (De Ritis ratio). Isolated AST rise: check CK — likely muscle source, not liver. Less liver-specific than ALT. Troponin more specific for MI than AST.

cards.title: ALP (Alkaline Phosphatase)
cards.summary: Isolated ALP: check GGT. GGT ↑ = liver origin. GGT normal = BONE origin (Paget's, bone mets). ALP + GGT + bilirubin ↑ = cholestatic pattern. Pregnancy ALP = placental isoenzyme — completely physiologi

cards.title: GGT (Gamma-Glutamyl Transferase)
cards.summary: GGT is the ALCOHOL marker — rises with regular drinking even before liver damage. Confirms liver origin of raised ALP. Normalises 2–6 weeks after stopping alcohol. Very sensitive but non-specific — el

cards.title: Bilirubin (Total)
cards.summary: Visible jaundice >35 µmol/L. Unconjugated ↑ = pre-hepatic (haemolysis) or Gilbert's — urine bilirubin ABSENT. Conjugated ↑ = hepatic or post-hepatic — bilirubin appears in urine (dark urine). Always c

cards.title: Albumin
cards.summary: Half-life ~20 days — reflects CHRONIC synthetic function. Acute illness lowers albumin non-specifically (negative APP). Used in Child-Pugh score. Check urine ACR if albumin low — could be nephrotic sy

cards.title: PT / INR
cards.summary: BEST acute synthetic function marker (factor VII t½ = 6 hrs — responds within hours). Prolonged PT that CORRECTS with Vit K = Vit K deficiency (fat malabsorption). Does NOT correct = liver failure. Cr

cards.title: LDH (Lactate Dehydrogenase)
cards.summary: Non-specific but essential. Haemolysis triad: ↑LDH + ↑unconjugated bilirubin + ↓haptoglobin. Very high LDH in lymphoma = adverse prognosis (IPI score). Also elevated in PE — non-specific marker.

cards.title: Haptoglobin
cards.summary: Low haptoglobin = haemolysis until proven otherwise. Consumed binding free Hb in intravascular haemolysis. Interpret with LDH + bilirubin. Absent in neonates — not useful in newborns. Normal haptoglob

cards.title: Total Protein
cards.summary: Total protein = albumin + globulins. High total protein + low albumin = high globulins → send SPEP/SIFE + serum free light chains (myeloma screen). Globulin gap >4 g/dL is abnormal — always investigat

cards.title: Creatinine
cards.summary: Does NOT rise until ~50% nephron loss. Always compare to BASELINE. Trend > single value. Muscle-dependent — a fit 25-year-old male with creatinine 115 is very different from a frail 80-year-old woman.

cards.title: Urea (BUN)
cards.summary: Urea:Creatinine ratio (µmol:µmol) >100:1 = pre-renal or UPPER GI BLEED. <100:1 = intrinsic or post-renal. Low urea + raised creatinine = liver failure context. In GI bleed: Urea rises disproportionate

cards.title: eGFR
cards.summary: CKD-EPI accounts for age, sex, race, creatinine. Use for CKD STAGING. Drug dose adjustments: metformin stop at G3b; many antibiotics (gentamicin, vancomycin), anticoagulants need adjustment at G3+. eG

cards.title: Sodium (Na⁺)
cards.summary: ASSESS VOLUME STATUS FIRST. Hypovolaemic = diuretics/Addison's. Euvolaemic = SIADH. Hypervolaemic = CCF/cirrhosis/nephrotic. Correct Na slowly (<10 mmol/L per 24 hrs) to avoid central pontine myelinol

cards.title: Potassium (K⁺)
cards.summary: K+ >6.5 = CARDIAC EMERGENCY. ECG: peaked T → PR prolongation → wide QRS → sine wave → VF. TREAT: Calcium gluconate FIRST (membrane stabilisation, 10 mins), then insulin+dextrose, salbutamol neb, sodiu

cards.title: Bicarbonate (HCO₃⁻)
cards.summary: Low HCO₃ → calculate ANION GAP: Na - (Cl + HCO₃). Normal AG = 8–12. High AG (>12) = MUDPILES. Normal AG acidosis = RTA, diarrhoea, saline excess (hyperchloraemic). Use delta-delta ratio to check if mi

cards.title: Phosphate (PO₄³⁻)
cards.summary: Refeeding syndrome: severe hypophosphataemia after refeeding a malnourished patient — can cause cardiac arrest and respiratory failure. Monitor electrolytes closely in all refeeding protocols. CKD: hi

cards.title: Calcium (Corrected Ca²⁺)
cards.summary: CORRECT for albumin: Adjusted Ca = measured Ca + 0.02 × (40 − albumin). Symptoms: Bones-Groans-Stones-Psychic moans. Ca >3.5 = crisis: rehydrate IV + bisphosphonate. Check PTH to differentiate primary

cards.title: Uric Acid (Urate)
cards.summary: Single raised urate does NOT diagnose gout — need clinical features or synovial fluid (negatively birefringent needle crystals under polarised light). Tumour lysis syndrome: rising K + PO₄ + urate + L

cards.title: Serum Osmolality
cards.summary: Osmolar gap = measured − calculated [2×Na + glucose + urea]. Gap >10 = toxic alcohol (methanol, ethylene glycol, ethanol, propylene glycol). Essential in any unexplained high AG metabolic acidosis. Al

cards.title: Chloride (Cl⁻)
cards.summary: Essential for anion gap calculation. Hyperchloraemia + low HCO₃ + normal AG = hyperchloraemic metabolic acidosis → think RTA or diarrhoea or excess saline. Hypochloraemia + high HCO₃ = metabolic alkal

cards.title: Protein (Dipstick / ACR)
cards.summary: ACR >3 mg/mmol = microalbuminuria (early diabetic nephropathy). ACR >30 = macroalbuminuria. Dipstick misses Bence Jones protein — send urine electrophoresis if myeloma suspected. Transient proteinuria

cards.title: Blood (Haematuria)
cards.summary: ALWAYS send for MC&S AND cytology if unexpected haematuria. RBC casts = glomerulonephritis (nephritic syndrome). Persistent unexplained haematuria in >40yrs = urology referral (2-week wait, rule out m

cards.title: Leucocytes & Nitrites
cards.summary: Nitrites negative does NOT exclude UTI — Gram-positive organisms (Enterococcus, Staph) don't produce nitrites. Leucocytes alone = non-specific. Sterile pyuria = TB ★, interstitial nephritis, urethriti

cards.title: Glucose (Glucosuria)
cards.summary: Glucosuria = blood glucose exceeded renal threshold (normally ~10 mmol/L). Normal urine glucose DOES NOT exclude diabetes — threshold varies. Glucosuria with normal blood glucose = renal tubular defec

cards.title: Ketones
cards.summary: In suspected DKA: check urine ketones AND serum beta-hydroxybutyrate (more sensitive/specific). Urine ketones are acetoacetate — serum is BHB. Euglycaemic DKA (SGLT2 inhibitors, pregnancy): ketones el

cards.title: Bilirubin & Urobilinogen
cards.summary: Urine bilirubin present = conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia — liver disease or obstruction. Absent bilirubin + high urobilinogen = pre-hepatic jaundice (haemolysis, Gilbert's). Absent both = complete obs

cards.title: pH & Specific Gravity
cards.summary: SG reflects renal concentrating ability. Fixed SG ~1.010 = isosthenuria = severely impaired concentrating ability (CKD, ATN). Inability to concentrate urine (SG <1.010 despite dehydration) = DI or tub

cards.title: ACR & PCR (Quantitative Proteinuria)
cards.summary: ACR preferred over 24-hr urine collection — equally accurate, far more practical. ACR >3 in diabetics = start ACE-i/ARB regardless of BP. Rising ACR in CKD = poor prognosis. Nephrotic syndrome: oedema

cards.title: Urine MC&S (Microscopy, Culture & Sensitivity)
cards.summary: Urine casts are formed in renal tubules — always pathological except hyaline casts. RBC casts = active glomerular disease (IgA nephropathy, post-streptococcal GN, vasculitis). Contaminated MSU: >2 org

cards.title: Total Cholesterol
cards.summary: Total cholesterol alone is insufficient — always interpret with full lipid profile. Non-HDL cholesterol (TC − HDL) is a better CV risk predictor than LDL alone. Calculate QRISK3 score to guide statin

cards.title: LDL Cholesterol
cards.summary: LDL is the PRIMARY treatment target for cardiovascular risk reduction. Goal depends on risk category. ASCVD/very high risk: LDL <1.4 mmol/L. Primary prevention high risk: <1.8. For FH: genetic testing

cards.title: HDL Cholesterol
cards.summary: HDL is PROTECTIVE — higher is better. Low HDL + high triglycerides = atherogenic dyslipidaemia (classic metabolic syndrome pattern). HDL <1.0 = significant cardiovascular risk. Raising HDL alone (niac

cards.title: Triglycerides
cards.summary: TG >10 mmol/L = risk of acute pancreatitis — start fibrates urgently. TG >5.6 = secondary causes must be excluded (DM, hypothyroidism, alcohol, drugs). Fasting sample essential — TG rises significantl

cards.title: Non-HDL Cholesterol
cards.summary: Non-HDL = Total cholesterol − HDL. Includes VLDL, IDL, LDL — all atherogenic particles. Better predictor of CV events than LDL alone, especially in diabetics and hypertriglyceridaemia. Now preferred t

cards.title: Lipoprotein(a) — Lp(a)
cards.summary: Lp(a) is an independent, inherited CV risk factor. Measure ONCE in lifetime (genetic — doesn't change with lifestyle). High Lp(a) >50 mg/dL = significantly elevated CV risk — consider more aggressive

cards.title: Fasting Plasma Glucose
cards.summary: Two abnormal fasting readings = diabetes (if asymptomatic). One reading + symptoms = sufficient for diagnosis. FPG 6.1–6.9 = impaired fasting glucose (IFG) — intermediate hyperglycaemia, high risk of

cards.title: HbA1c
cards.summary: HbA1c reflects average glucose over past 2–3 months (RBC lifespan). UNRELIABLE in: haemoglobinopathy, haemolysis, pregnancy, CKD — use fasting glucose or 2-hr OGTT instead. Treatment targets: T1DM = 4

cards.title: 2-hr OGTT (75g Oral Glucose Tolerance Test)
cards.summary: Gold standard for gestational diabetes screening (GDM). Also used to diagnose IGT when other tests borderline. Patient must eat carbohydrates for 3 days before test. In pregnancy: any GDM = strict glu

cards.title: Random Plasma Glucose
cards.summary: Random glucose ≥11.1 + symptoms (polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss) = diabetes diagnosed without further testing. Random glucose ≥11.1 without symptoms = repeat test needed. Critical values: <2.2 mmol

cards.title: Serum Insulin & C-peptide
cards.summary: C-peptide differentiates exogenous insulin abuse (low C-peptide) from insulinoma (high C-peptide). In T1DM: C-peptide confirms beta cell failure — useful when diagnosis uncertain (LADA vs T2DM). Durin

cards.title: Beta-hydroxybutyrate (BHB)
cards.summary: BHB is preferred over urine ketones in DKA monitoring (more accurate, faster, bedside). DKA resolution criteria: BHB <0.6 mmol/L + glucose <14 + HCO₃ >18. Euglycaemic DKA (SGLT2i): BHB elevated but gl

cards.title: Glucose Monitoring — Targets
cards.summary: Hypoglycaemia treatment: if conscious = 15–20g fast-acting carb (glucose gel, orange juice, Lucozade) → recheck at 15 mins → repeat if still <4. If unconscious = IV dextrose (10% preferred over 50%) o

cards.title: Serum Calcium (Corrected)
cards.summary: ALWAYS correct: Adjusted Ca = measured Ca + 0.02 × (40 − albumin g/L). Symptoms: Bones (pain/fractures), Groans (nausea/constipation), Stones (renal calculi), Psychic moans (depression/confusion/coma)

cards.title: Serum Phosphate
cards.summary: Hyperphosphataemia in CKD drives secondary hyperparathyroidism and vascular calcification. Treat with dietary restriction + phosphate binders (sevelamer, calcium carbonate, lanthanum). Hypophosphataem

cards.title: PTH (Parathyroid Hormone)
cards.summary: KEY interpretation: Ca ↑ + PTH ↑ (or inappropriately normal) = PRIMARY hyperparathyroidism. Ca ↑ + PTH ↓ = malignancy or Vit D toxicity. Ca ↓ + PTH ↑ = secondary HPT (CKD/Vit D def). Ca ↓ + PTH ↓ = hy

cards.title: Vitamin D (25-OH Vitamin D)
cards.summary: 25-OH Vit D is the storage form — measure this. 1,25-OH (calcitriol) is active form — measure in sarcoid/granulomatous disease (produced ectopically). Deficiency: rickets (children), osteomalacia (adu

cards.title: ALP — Bone Fraction
cards.summary: Paget's disease: ALP can be extremely elevated (>1000 U/L) with completely normal GGT — characteristic pattern. Treatment with bisphosphonates normalises ALP. Bone ALP (isoenzyme) can separate bone fr

cards.title: DEXA Scan — T-score (Bone Density)
cards.summary: T-score compares to young healthy adult peak bone mass. Z-score compares to age-matched peers (use in premenopausal women and men <50). FRAX tool: integrates clinical risk factors + BMD to calculate 1

cards.title: CSF Opening Pressure
cards.summary: Always perform CT head before LP if: papilloedema, focal neurology, GCS <13, immunocompromised, new-onset seizures. IIH: young obese woman, headache, papilloedema, normal MRI — opening pressure >25 cm

cards.title: CSF Protein
cards.summary: Protein elevated in virtually all serious CNS infections. Albumino-cytological dissociation = high protein + normal cell count = Guillain-Barré ★. Very high protein + sticky/xanthochromic CSF = TB men

cards.title: CSF Glucose
cards.summary: ALWAYS take serum glucose simultaneously. Low CSF glucose = bacteria or TB consuming glucose. Ratio <0.4 = very strong indicator of bacterial meningitis. Normal ratio with high WBC = viral meningitis.

cards.title: CSF Cell Count
cards.summary: Cell type is THE KEY distinguisher: neutrophils = bacterial (treat immediately — do NOT wait for culture). Lymphocytes = viral or TB. In bacterial meningitis: treat empirically with ceftriaxone + amox

cards.title: CSF Xanthochromia & RBCs
cards.summary: If CT head negative but clinical suspicion of SAH: MUST perform LP >2 hrs after symptom onset. Send for spectrophotometry (not just visual inspection). Xanthochromia persists up to 2 weeks. Distinguis

cards.title: PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen)
cards.summary: PSA is NOT a perfect screening test. Sensitivity ~70%, specificity ~60%. Many false positives (prostatitis, BPH) and false negatives. Free:total PSA ratio: <15% = more likely malignant. PSA velocity (

cards.title: AFP (Alpha-Fetoprotein)
cards.summary: HCC surveillance: 6-monthly AFP + ultrasound in cirrhotic patients. AFP >400 = likely HCC if liver mass present. Normal AFP does NOT exclude HCC (20% of HCC don't produce AFP). In testicular cancer: A

cards.title: CEA (Carcinoembryonic Antigen)
cards.summary: CEA should NOT be used for colorectal cancer screening — it has poor sensitivity for early disease. Main use: POST-RESECTION monitoring for recurrence. Rising CEA after curative resection = recurrence

cards.title: CA 19-9
cards.summary: CA 19-9 is elevated by BILIARY OBSTRUCTION regardless of malignancy — uninformative with jaundice until obstruction relieved. Use to monitor known pancreatic cancer response to treatment. Pre-operativ

cards.title: CA 125
cards.summary: CA 125 alone is not a screening test for ovarian cancer. Use with USS in post-menopausal women with symptoms. RMI (Risk of Malignancy Index) = CA125 × USS score × menopausal status. RMI >200 = refer t

cards.title: Beta-hCG
cards.summary: Testicular cancer staging uses AFP + β-hCG + LDH together (S-stage in TNM). Pure seminoma can produce β-hCG (never AFP). Choriocarcinoma: extremely high β-hCG, highly chemotherapy-responsive — do NOT

cards.title: CA 15-3 & CEA — Breast
cards.summary: CA 15-3 is used for MONITORING metastatic breast cancer, not for diagnosis or screening. Rising CA 15-3 in known breast cancer = disease progression (confirm with imaging before changing treatment). U

cards.title: LDH — Haematological Malignancy
cards.summary: In lymphoma: LDH is part of the International Prognostic Index (IPI). Elevated LDH = advanced disease, worse prognosis. Serial LDH used to monitor treatment response — falling LDH = responding. In hae

cards.title: Tumour Markers — Combined Use
cards.summary: MUST measure AFP, β-hCG, and LDH BEFORE orchiectomy (staging purpose) and AFTER (half-life assessment). AFP half-life = 5–7 days. β-hCG half-life = 24–36 hrs. Markers not falling appropriately post-op

cards.title: pH
cards.summary: pH <7.35 = acidaemia. pH >7.45 = alkalaemia. Normal pH does NOT mean normal — compensation can mask mixed disorders. Always interpret pH with PaCO₂ and HCO₃ together. Step 1: Is it acidosis or alkalos

cards.title: PaCO₂
cards.summary: PaCO₂ = respiratory component. ↑ PaCO₂ + ↓ pH = respiratory acidosis. ↓ PaCO₂ + ↑ pH = respiratory alkalosis. Winter's formula for expected compensation in metabolic acidosis: PaCO₂ = (1.5 × HCO₃) + 8

cards.title: PaO₂
cards.summary: P/F ratio (PaO₂/FiO₂): >400 = normal. 200–400 = mild ARDS. 100–200 = moderate ARDS. <100 = severe ARDS. A-a gradient = PAO₂ − PaO₂. Normal A-a = Age/4 + 4 mmHg. Elevated A-a gradient = V/Q mismatch or

cards.title: HCO₃ (Bicarbonate)
cards.summary: Anion gap (AG) = Na − (Cl + HCO₃). Normal = 8–12 mmol/L. High AG metabolic acidosis mnemonic MUDPILES: Methanol, Uraemia, DKA ★, Propylene glycol, Isoniazid/Iron, Lactic acidosis ★, Ethylene glycol, S

cards.title: Base Excess (BE) & Lactate
cards.summary: Base deficit <−6 in trauma = significant shock — predicts need for massive transfusion. Lactate ≥2 = hyperlactataemia (monitor, investigate cause). Lactate ≥4 = septic shock criterion → 30mL/kg IV flu

cards.title: PT / INR
cards.summary: INR monitors extrinsic pathway. INR >1.5 = significant coagulopathy. Warfarin reversal: INR 3–5 (no bleed) = hold dose. INR >5 or bleeding = Vitamin K IV + PCC (4-factor) preferred over FFP. Do NOT gi

cards.title: APTT
cards.summary: APTT monitors intrinsic pathway. Lupus anticoagulant: APTT prolonged but causes THROMBOSIS not bleeding — mixing study fails to correct (inhibitor). Confirm with DRVVT. LMWH does NOT prolong APTT — mo

cards.title: D-Dimer
cards.summary: D-dimer = RULE-OUT test. Negative D-dimer + low pre-test probability (Wells score) = DVT/PE safely excluded. Age-adjusted cut-off (age × 0.01 µg/mL in >50y) reduces unnecessary CT-PA by 30%. NEVER use

cards.title: Fibrinogen & TT
cards.summary: Fibrinogen <1.5 g/L = give cryoprecipitate (10 units raises fibrinogen ~1 g/L). Fibrinogen <1.0 g/L = emergency cryoprecipitate. In obstetric haemorrhage: fibrinogen <2.0 = early predictor of severity

cards.title: Anti-Xa Level
cards.summary: Anti-Xa is the CORRECT monitoring test for LMWH (not APTT). Monitor in: extremes of weight (<45kg or >100kg), renal impairment (CrCl <30), pregnancy, neonates. Prophylactic LMWH target: 0.2–0.4 IU/mL.

cards.title: High-Sensitivity Troponin (hs-cTn)
cards.summary: 0h/1h algorithm: if hs-cTnT <5 ng/L at 0h AND delta <3 ng/L at 1h = 99.6% NPV for NSTEMI (rule-out). Delta (absolute rise) is MORE important than single value. Peak troponin 12–24h post-MI. Renal fail

cards.title: BNP / NT-proBNP
cards.summary: NT-proBNP rule-in by age: <50y = 450 pg/mL. 50–75y = 900 pg/mL. >75y = 1800 pg/mL. Negative BNP/NT-proBNP has high NPV for HF (~95% — useful to exclude in acute dyspnoea). Obese patients: lower NT-pro

cards.title: CK-MB & LDH
cards.summary: CK-MB still useful for: (1) Detecting re-infarction — returns to normal faster than troponin, so new CK-MB rise after normalisation = re-MI. (2) Estimating infarct size (peak correlates with area of n

cards.title: ANA (Antinuclear Antibody)
cards.summary: ANA is a SCREENING test only. Positive ANA → ENA panel: anti-dsDNA (SLE specific ★), anti-Sm (SLE specific), anti-Ro/La (Sjögren, neonatal lupus), anti-Scl-70 (scleroderma), anti-centromere (CREST), a

cards.title: Anti-dsDNA & Anti-Sm
cards.summary: Anti-dsDNA is the MOST USEFUL antibody in SLE monitoring. Serial titres + C3/C4 guide flare prediction. Anti-dsDNA + ↓ complement + active urine sediment = lupus nephritis → kidney biopsy urgently. An

cards.title: Anti-TPO (Anti-Thyroid Peroxidase) ★
cards.summary: Anti-TPO confirms AUTOIMMUNE thyroid aetiology. Positive anti-TPO in euthyroid patient = 4× increased hypothyroidism risk per decade — recheck TSH annually. In pregnancy: positive anti-TPO + TSH >2.5

cards.title: Anti-TG & TRAb
cards.summary: Anti-TG critical in thyroid cancer: elevated anti-TG falsely LOWERS serum thyroglobulin (Tg) — making cancer recurrence monitoring unreliable. Always check anti-TG alongside Tg post-thyroidectomy. If

cards.title: ANCA (cANCA / pANCA)
cards.summary: ANCA-associated vasculitis (AAV) triad: small vessel vasculitis + ANCA + end-organ damage. Renal emergency: haematuria + proteinuria + crescentic GN + positive ANCA → immunosuppression immediately (ri

cards.title: RF & Anti-CCP
cards.summary: Anti-CCP (anti-cyclic citrullinated peptide) is the most specific test for RA (98%). Anti-CCP + inflammatory polyarthritis = RA until proven otherwise → refer rheumatology, start DMARDs early. High an

cards.title: Complement C3 & C4
cards.summary: Complement pattern interpretation: C3↓ + C4↓ = classic pathway (SLE ★, lupus nephritis, cryoglobulinaemia). C3↓ + C4 normal = alternative pathway (MPGN II, C3 nephritic factor). C4↓ + C3 normal = C1-i

cards.title: Antiphospholipid Antibodies (APLA)
cards.summary: APS diagnosis requires: (1) Clinical: thrombosis or pregnancy morbidity (3+ miscarriages <10wk, 1+ loss >10wk, premature birth <34wk for pre-eclampsia). (2) Lab: positive on 2 occasions ≥12 weeks apar

cards.title: Cortisol & ACTH
cards.summary: Cushing's screening: 1mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test (normal = AM cortisol <50 nmol/L). OR 24h urine free cortisol. OR late-night salivary cortisol (best test — loss of diurnal nadir). Ad

cards.title: TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone)
cards.summary: TSH = most sensitive thyroid test. Start with TSH alone. If abnormal → add FT4 (+ FT3 if TSH suppressed). TSH 4–10 mIU/L (subclinical hypothyroid): treat if symptomatic, anti-TPO positive, or pregnant

cards.title: Prolactin
cards.summary: PRL >5000 mIU/L = prolactinoma very likely. PRL 1000–5000 = could be drug-induced, hypothyroidism, or macroprolactinaemia. Always exclude: pregnancy + hypothyroidism + medications FIRST. Macroprolacti

cards.title: LH / FSH & Testosterone
cards.summary: FSH >40 IU/L + amenorrhoea ≥12 months in <40y = POI — HRT essential (osteoporosis + cardiovascular protection). LH:FSH >2:1 in PCOS (60% of cases — not a diagnostic criterion). Morning testosterone (7

cards.title: IGF-1 & Growth Hormone
cards.summary: IGF-1 is BEST screening test for acromegaly (stable throughout day, unlike GH which is pulsatile). Elevated IGF-1 → confirm with OGTT (GH measured at 0, 30, 60, 90, 120 min — normal = suppression to <

cards.title: Digoxin
cards.summary: ECG of digoxin effect (not toxicity): reversed tick ST depression (scooping), short QT. Toxicity ECG: bradycardia, AV block, any arrhythmia. Treatment: stop digoxin, correct K⁺ to >4.0 mmol/L, anti-di

cards.title: Lithium
cards.summary: Narrowest therapeutic window of all psychiatric drugs. Monitor pre-treatment (U&E, TFTs, ECG, pregnancy test) then every 6 months. NSAIDs are the most common cause of iatrogenic lithium toxicity — edu

cards.title: Vancomycin
cards.summary: AUC/MIC monitoring now preferred over trough-only (reduces toxicity). Target AUC 400–600 for serious MRSA. Monitor creatinine daily during therapy. In renal impairment: extend dosing interval (not red

cards.title: Phenytoin & Valproate
cards.summary: Phenytoin: non-linear kinetics — small dose ↑ causes disproportionately large level rise. In hypoalbuminaemia: corrected phenytoin = measured ÷ [(albumin g/dL × 0.1) + 0.1]. Major interactions: induce

cards.title: Gentamicin & Aminoglycosides
cards.summary: Once-daily gentamicin: 5–7 mg/kg/day (Hartford nomogram for renal dosing). Pre-dose trough MUST be <1 mg/L before next dose (drug-free interval prevents nephrotoxicity). Monitor creatinine DAILY. Stop

cards.title: Critical Values — Haematology
cards.summary: Laboratory critical values: call ordering physician within 60 minutes. Document: who notified, time, read-back of result. Patient must be reviewed within 1 hour. Do NOT simply result and walk away. Re

cards.title: Critical Values — Biochemistry
cards.summary: Hyperkalaemia emergency (K >6.5): Step 1 — IV calcium gluconate 10mL 10% over 10 min (membrane stabilisation, IMMEDIATE). Step 2 — IV insulin 10U + 50mL 50% dextrose (shifts K into cells, onset 20 min

cards.title: Critical Values — Microbiology
cards.summary: Blood culture Gram stain guide: Gram-positive cocci clusters = MRSA/MSSA → vancomycin (MRSA) or flucloxacillin (MSSA). Gram-positive cocci chains = Streptococcus/Enterococcus → benzylpenicillin or amo

Abdominal Mud Pack
cards.summary: Most commonly prescribed mud application in BNYS. Indication: constipation, IBS, indigestion, hyperacidity. Apply cool mud pack from navel to pubic symphysis for 20–30 minutes, morning before breakfas

cards.title: Breath-Holding Time (BHT)
cards.summary: Simple clinical marker of respiratory and cardiovascular health. Normal: 25–40 seconds. <20 sec = significant dysfunction (poor parasympathetic tone). >40 sec = good cardiovascular fitness. Measure: n

cards.title: Yoga Nidra — Therapeutic Goldmine
cards.summary: 30 minutes = 2–3 hours of deep sleep physiologically. Induces theta brainwave state (4–8 Hz). Evidence: ↓ cortisol, ↓ sympathetic tone, ↑ DHEA, ↓ PTSD symptoms, ↓ chronic pain scores. Prescription: 30

cards.title: BATHE Technique — 5-min Psychosocial Screen

cards.title: The 90-Second Rule
cards.summary: Doctors interrupt patients on average within 18 seconds. If allowed to speak uninterrupted, 80% finish in under 90 seconds — and reveal the real reason for the consultation. The first 90 seconds shoul

cards.title: Sequence of Yoga Practice
cards.summary: Correct order: Asanas → Pranayama → Meditation/Yoga Nidra → Shavasana. Never reverse. Asanas prepare the body for pranayama; pranayama prepares the mind for meditation. Pranayama on an unprepared body

cards.title: Alkaline vs Acid-Forming Foods
cards.summary: Alkaline-forming (eat freely): all fruits (even citrus — alkaline ASH), all vegetables, sprouts, millet, seeds, nuts, coconut water. Acid-forming (limit): meat, fish, eggs, dairy, refined grains, suga

cards.title: Healing Crisis During Fasting
cards.summary: Expect on days 1–2: headache, fatigue, irritability, bad breath, coated tongue, body odour — signs of active detoxification, not side effects. Dangerous symptoms: severe hypoglycaemia, cardiac palpita

cards.title: Deqi Sensation in Acupressure
cards.summary: The therapeutic sensation at a correctly stimulated acupoint: dull ache, heaviness, tingling, warmth, or electric-like sensation radiating along the meridian. Absence of Deqi = incorrect location, ins

cards.title: Self-Abhyanga Prescription

cards.title: Documentation Principle
cards.summary: 'If it isn't written, it didn't happen.' Good documentation protects the patient (continuity of care), protects you (medico-legal), and improves outcomes. Write legibly in black ink. Date and sign eve

cards.title: Inspection Before Palpation
cards.summary: Always. The end-of-bed assessment gives you the gestalt of how sick the patient is. A patient who looks well despite abnormal observations is different from one who looks unwell with normal observatio

cards.title: JVP — Window to the Right Atrium
cards.summary: A raised JVP is one of the earliest signs of right heart failure. In cardiac tamponade: Beck's triad — raised JVP + hypotension + muffled heart sounds. Pulsus paradoxus also present. Always check JVP

cards.title: Therapeutic Oils in BNYS
cards.summary: Sesame oil: warming, anti-Vata, best for dry skin and joints. Coconut oil: cooling, anti-inflammatory, best for Pitta and skin. Mustard oil: deeply warming — cold/flu, arthritis. Neem oil: antimicrobi

cards.title: Ideal Mud for Therapy
cards.summary: Best therapeutic mud: black cotton soil or fullers earth (multani mitti) — high mineral content, fine texture. Collect from 3–4 feet depth (free of contaminants). Sun-dry, powder, sieve, store in eart
